title: [phase:plan] [family:github-implement-path] [provider_hint:codex] Analyze how to redesign the GitHub implementation path so that /vote-level work keeps a Codex+Claude+GLM council active through implementation completion instead of collapsing to Codex-only. Focus on scripts/gha24 and .github/workflow...
summary_text: - Current gap: [`scripts/gha24`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/gha24#L343) preserves main/assist intent and council-style protocol in the issue, but [`fugue-tutti-caller.yml`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L744) forwards only scalar execution settings, and [`fugue-codex-implement.yml`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-codex-implement.yml#L641) rebuilds implementation as Codex-only preflight plus one Codex executor. - Minimum viable redesign: keep Codex as the only writer, but pass a `council_manifest` from `/vote` into implement and require three implementation roles to stay provider-backed: Codex `implementer`, Claude `critic`, GLM `reviewer/reliability`; Codex applies changes only after a council checkpoint, not after a solo loop. - Workflow touchpoints: extend [`scripts/gha24`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/gha24#L367) to stamp explicit council intent in the issue body, add new `workflow_call` inputs/outputs in [`fugue-codex-implement.yml`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/.github/workflows/fugue-codex-implement.yml#L1) for resolved providers/lane manifest, replace the current Codex-only `preflight_parallel` block with provider-aware council subjobs, and gate PR creation/finalize on a final council verdict instead of `codex_exit` alone. - Test touchpoints: extend [`scripts/check-agent-matrix-parity.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-agent-matrix-parity.sh), [`tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/tests/test-resolve-orchestration-context.sh), and [`scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/scripts/check-codex-vote-prompt.sh) so `/vote` handoff proves the trio survives into implement; add a new workflow contract test asserting `fugue-codex-implement.yml` refuses to run in “council mode” unless Codex+Claude+GLM evidence artifacts are present. - Main risk: once implementation depends on the same trio as `/vote`, provider availability becomes a completion dependency, so without a clear degraded-mode policy the system can deadlock or silently reintroduce Codex-only fallbacks under failure pressure.
